steps.title: Identify what verb form the sentence requires
steps.content: The sentence reads: she [blank] her fingers across the harp strings and creates a radiant sound. The subject is she (3rd person singular) and the paired verb creates is simple present. The blank must match both the subject and the tense.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong forms and confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• "sweep": This is the base form with no 3rd person singular ending. It does not agree with the subject she. ✗
  • "are sweeping": This is 1st/3rd person plural present progressive. She is singular, so this doesn't agree. ✗
  • "were sweeping": This is past progressive, mismatching the present tense of creates. ✗
  • "sweeps": Simple present, 3rd person singular — matches she and is parallel with creates. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
